instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md, an agent knows the interface, schema, constraints, and objective but not how to construct a feasible optimal assignment; the environment evaluator, docs, and runtime behavior are essential, so the instruction is not a recipe.

discovery_budget.discovery: A leaf's effective memory.min is bounded by every ancestor and proportionally down-scaled when siblings oversubscribe, so floors must be reserved at every level without oversubscribing any delegated budget.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Stating the reservation algorithm would turn the floor family into transcription instead of forcing the agent to reason about propagation.

discovery_budget.discovery: Each node's reclaimable budget for the best-effort tier is its delegated budget minus the min it reserved, so the fairness allocation is entangled with the floor reservation.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Revealing how to compute and split reclaimable budget would hand over the coupling that makes the objective hard.

discovery_budget.discovery: The worst-leaf low ratio is capped by the most-constrained subtree (the largest ratio every node can supply to its subtree out of its reclaimable budget), not by any single leaf.
discovery_budget.planned_location: internal/refeval/objective.go (independent optimum) mirrored in the Python verifier
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Spelling out the optimum as a formula/step list would collapse the search to plugging numbers.

discovery_budget.discovery: A leaf's global CPU share is the product of per-level normalized weights, so weights must be solved level by level, not set per leaf.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming the level-by-level construction would remove the path-product reasoning.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: Non-repair optimization shape: the instruction completely specifies the public target (interface, schema, the five constraints, the down-scaling and path-product rules, the reclaimable-budget definition, the objective, and the tolerance) while withholding the construction algorithm and the solver-stage locations.

attack_path.summary: Read the model, reserve floors bottom-up, set decreasing maxima with headroom, solve weights per level, allocate io within device pools, then water-fill reclaimable budget to the binding subtree; self-check with the reference evaluator.
attack_path.reasoning: Each step is constrained by the others: floors set the reclaimable budget; maxima bound headroom; weights must compose to the leaf share; the objective is bounded by feasibility. A competent engineer follows this path but must derive each construction from the documented semantics.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Implement the five solver-stage bodies so the emitted assignment is feasible on every family and the worst-leaf low ratio reaches the optimum.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: There is no smaller patch: stubbing any one stage drops its constraint family or the objective below tolerance, and the optimum requires the binding-subtree reasoning, so the genuine change is several hundred lines of coordinated logic.

collapse_audit.reasoning: After full disclosure the residual work is the constructive coordinated allocation and the binding-subtree optimum, recomputed against verify-time-generated trees by an independent verifier; no surface signal shortcuts it.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: Coordinating floor reservation, reclaimable-budget fairness, max/headroom limits, path-product weights, and device pools across the whole tree so that the assignment is feasible and reaches the independently computed optimum on unseen trees.

topology_enumeration.summary: Bottom-up floor reservation with reclaimable water-filling.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Floors reserved in qa determine the reclaimable budget qe water-fills, and the objective is scored against the optimum in objective.go; missing any one yields infeasibility or a sub-optimal ratio.

topology_enumeration.summary: Level-by-level weight solving composed into leaf shares.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Per-level weights must be derived from the tree structure in types and the path-product rule in docs; a per-leaf weight ignores sibling normalization and fails the share tolerance.
Max-hierarchy/headroom in qb and device pools in qd constrain the region qe optimizes; an objective step that ignores those produces an infeasible assignment.

symbol_table.purpose: reserves the protected memory floor at every node so effective floors survive down-scaling
sets the non-increasing upper limits and the throttle gap for each node
chooses integer relative weights per level so each leaf path-product meets its target
allocates per-device throughput caps within each pool above each leaf floor
distributes the best-effort reclaimable tier to maximize the worst-off subtree ratio

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: renders an ASCII summary of a tree for dry-run console output
aggregates per-node counters for the documentation report only
formats device pool labels for diagnostic listings off the assignment path

renames_during_drafting.reason: a floor-reservation function named after the protected-floor concept would telegraph the fix; renamed to an opaque per-package Apply.
a weight-solving function named after cpu weight would collide with the instruction noun; renamed to opaque Apply.
an objective function named after the fairness allocation would reveal the approach; renamed to opaque Apply.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: effective-protection down-scaling and reclaimable-budget definitions live in the reference evaluator and docs, not as a recipe in the instruction
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a solver can satisfy a configured floor while the effective floor is down-scaled to zero by an unfunded ancestor
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the down-scaling rule is documented and implemented in a readable evaluator the solver may call

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the mem_low fairness budget at each node depends on how much mem_min that node reserved, which depends on subtree floors
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: optimizing fairness independently of floor reservation overcommits a node or wastes budget, missing the optimum
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the reclaimable-budget definition is stated in the instruction and docs

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: per-leaf weights set to the global target look locally correct but miss the path-product after sibling normalization
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a locally plausible weight passes a single-node sanity check yet fails the global share tolerance
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the path-product rule and the [1,10000] integer range are stated in the instruction

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: scenario JSON, assignment JSON, report JSON, the Go evaluator, and the Python verifier must agree on the same numeric semantics
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a partial implementation handling one resource emits a report that round-trips but fails recomputation under the independent evaluator
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: every schema field, formula, and tolerance is visible in the instruction or docs

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: an assignment meeting the four feasibility families but splitting reclaimable budget evenly produces a feasible-looking report whose worst-leaf ratio is below the optimum
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: feasibility alone reads as success; the objective shortfall is only caught against the independently computed optimum
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the objective and within-tolerance acceptance are stated in the instruction

calibration_plan.human_sanity: a systems engineer can solve by reading the cgroup-v2 docs, reserving floors bottom-up, setting decreasing max with headroom, solving weights level by level, allocating io within device pools, and water-filling reclaimable budget to the binding subtree
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: tests regenerate assignments by running the binary, generate fresh trees at verify time, reject static report writes, recompute in Python instead of trusting the environment evaluator, and reject overcommitting assignments
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: stub each of the five solver stages independently and confirm at least one verifier test fails for that stage only

pass_rate_target.basis: stronger target agent; aligns with Part E Hard/Medium/Easy thresholds on worst/best model accuracy. Verifier is offline (pytest baked into the Dockerfile, no runtime installs under allow_internet = false) and post-upload difficulty is classified by Part E after platform agent runs.

metrics.criterion: effective floors, max hierarchy, headroom, path-product shares, and device sums hold on bundled and generated trees
the worst-leaf mem_low ratio reaches the independently computed optimum within tolerance and no node oversubscribes its reclaimable budget
re-running the solver on the same tree yields identical feasibility and objective and the suite leaves the binary and report in place
cgsolve, solve-one.sh, and run-suite.sh read the documented inputs and write the documented assignment/report shape
every node appears in the assignment with all six fields and every bundled scenario appears in the report

verifier_scoring_plan.binary_threshold_rule: reward is 1 only on pytest exit 0; otherwise reward is 0

subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: run-suite.sh regenerates one JSON report from bundled trees; verifier tests invoke cgsolve on temporary generated trees

platform_files.role: Edition 2 metadata and runtime limits; sets [environment] allow_internet = false
constraint-complete public contract for interface, schema, constraints, and objective
local output declaration for the generated report
verifier entrypoint; invokes pre-installed pytest only
independent Python verifier reimplementing the cgroup-v2 semantics
oracle that writes the five solver-stage bodies and rebuilds
digest-pinned offline Go image with pytest, tmux, asciinema
authoring metadata for frontier, base image, decoys, flipping point

task_files.role: entrypoint reading a scenario and writing an assignment
readable reference objective and independent optimum
runs the solver over all bundled trees and writes the report

fix_frontier.distribution: five solver stages across five opaque packages: memory-floor reservation, max/headroom limits, cpu path-product weights, io device allocation, reclaimable max-min fairness
fix_frontier.naming_policy: opaque package dirs qa-qe with one Apply function each; shared domain types in a non-fix package
fix_frontier.helpers_policy: sibling helper packages r6-r8 do diagnostic/formatting work that never touches the assignment

preferred_assertion_styles: recompute effective protections with an independent evaluator
recompute and compare the worst-leaf optimum within tolerance
run the binary on freshly generated trees
forbidden_assertion_styles: scenario to field to expected boolean tables

task_shape.collapse_risk: medium; mitigated by an independent verifier evaluator, verify-time-generated trees, a non-textbook protection-redistribution model, and a five-stage fix frontier

category_profile.challenge_family: constrained resource-budget allocation over a delegated cgroup hierarchy
category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: the run interface, the scenario/assignment/report schema, the five hard constraints, the cgroup-v2 down-scaling rule, the path-product cpu rule, the reclaimable-budget definition, the objective, and the within-tolerance acceptance
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: the solver-stage identifiers, the constructive allocation algorithm, the optimum as a recipe, per-scenario expected numbers, and which stage controls which test
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: the effective resource policy derives across delegated budgets, ancestor protection propagation, sibling weight normalization, and device pools; no single node assignment suffices and local choices break distant feasibility and the objective
category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: trusting the tamperable environment evaluator, accepting overcommitting assignments, brittle exact-value thresholds, or scenarios that allow a per-leaf shortcut
category_profile.coverage_role: adds the first Go optimization/search task and the first constrained-allocation challenge to an otherwise all-repair bank

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low: solver stages are opaque qa-qe and domain types live in a non-fix package
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low: the instruction states constraints and the objective but no per-scenario expected values; expectations are recomputed
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: low: the manifest fixes the five stage symbols and the oracle uses them verbatim
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: low: every tested interface, schema field, formula, and tolerance is in instruction.md or docs

actionability_plan.verifier_command_visible: instruction.md names /app/bin/cgsolve <in> <out>, bash /app/scripts/solve-one.sh <in> <out>, bash /app/scripts/run-suite.sh, and the pytest verifier command
actionability_plan.source_fix_intent_visible: instruction.md says solver sources live under /app/internal and /app/cmd (also via /app/environment) and static assignment writes are insufficient
actionability_plan.generated_output_rule_visible: instruction.md identifies /app/output/plan-report.json and per-call assignment outputs and that trees not under /app/data are also solved
actionability_plan.exact_formula_home: instruction.md and /app/docs state the down-scaling rule, path-product rule, reclaimable-budget definition, objective, and tolerance
actionability_plan.schema_home: instruction.md names every scenario, assignment, and report field; docs carry the worked semantics

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: no waiver is planned; any checker warning should be resolved or documented before packaging

reference_pattern.justification_if_none: Original synthetic task built from the cgroup-v2 memory-protection model and systemd delegated-slice budgeting; not derived from a website inspiration, a prior public task, or a template reskin. The reference library currently has no promoted entries.

realism_source.evidence_basis: Linux cgroup-v2 memory controller protection model (memory.min/memory.low proportional distribution and down-scaling, memory.high/memory.max), cpu.weight relative sibling distribution, io.max, and systemd delegated-slice budgeting
realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: protection-redistribution and delegation semantics come from the kernel cgroup-v2 documentation and systemd resource control; trees and the fairness objective are synthetic to keep data local and deterministic
realism_source.minimization_preserves: ancestor protection propagation with proportional down-scaling, no-overcommit of delegated budgets, max-hierarchy inheritance, relative cpu weights as path-products, device io pools, and reclaimable best-effort distribution
realism_source.synthetic_exception_review: not a synthetic exception; the model and constraints are drawn from a real subsystem, only the instances and objective threshold are generated
